description:
You are the systems administrator of several servers and need to prepare a very small “status-at-a-glance” report that managers can read.  
  Everything you need is already on disk.  Your job is to build the file  
  /home/user/reports/connection_load_summary.tsv  
  by extracting selected columns from two existing tab-separated log files and then gluing the results together.

  Source material (already on disk):

  1) /home/user/raw_logs/active_connections.tsv  
     – tab-separated, **five** columns, first row is a header  

        HOSTNAME<tab>IP<tab>PORT<tab>STATE<tab>PID  
        web01<tab>10.0.0.1<tab>80<tab>ESTABLISHED<tab>2345  
        db01<tab>10.0.0.2<tab>5432<tab>ESTABLISHED<tab>5432  
        cache01<tab>10.0.0.3<tab>6379<tab>LISTEN<tab>3333  

  2) /home/user/raw_logs/cpu_load.tsv  
     – tab-separated, **four** columns, first row is a header  

        HOSTNAME<tab>1min<tab>5min<tab>15min  
        web01<tab>0.10<tab>0.12<tab>0.15  
        db01<tab>0.55<tab>0.53<tab>0.50  
        cache01<tab>0.20<tab>0.25<tab>0.30  

  What the report must look like (exact byte-for-byte target; “<tab>” means an ASCII TAB 0x09):  

        HOSTNAME<tab>PORT<tab>STATE<tab>1min_LOAD
        web01<tab>80<tab>ESTABLISHED<tab>0.10
        db01<tab>5432<tab>ESTABLISHED<tab>0.55
        cache01<tab>6379<tab>LISTEN<tab>0.20

  Notes on building the report:
  • Use standard coreutils tools only (cut, paste, sort, grep, etc.).  
  • You may create intermediate temporary files if you wish, but remove them afterwards.  
  • Do **not** re-type the data manually; extract it with column manipulation commands.  
  • Preserve the order of rows exactly as they appear in active_connections.tsv.  
  • The column to pull from each source file is as follows  
      – active_connections.tsv : 1 (HOSTNAME), 3 (PORT), 4 (STATE)  
      – cpu_load.tsv           : 2 (1min)  
    Rename the cpu_load column to 1min_LOAD in the final header.  

  Command logging requirement:  
  While you work, append every command you actually execute (in the exact form you typed it) to  
  /home/user/reports/summary_commands.log  
  Put one command per line, in execution order, with no extra commentary.  
  This allows an auditor to replay your steps later.

  Summary of what must exist when you are done:

  1) /home/user/reports/connection_load_summary.tsv – containing exactly the three data lines plus the header shown above.  
  2) /home/user/reports/summary_commands.log        – a plain-text list of all commands you issued, each on its own line (at least three distinct commands: two separate cut commands and one paste command must appear somewhere in the list).

  The automated grader will check:

  • That the summary file exists at the exact path and matches the precise content and ordering above, byte-for-byte.  
  • That the commands log exists, is non-empty, and contains at minimum the keywords “cut” on two different lines and “paste” on at least one line.

  Do not move or rename any of the source log files.  
  Do not use sudo or elevated privileges; everything is writable by the current user.  
  Work only with what is described here and place nothing outside /home/user/reports.  
  When you’re convinced all requirements are satisfied, you can consider the task complete.
truth:
<!-- Files pre-populated before the agent starts -->
  <pre_existing_files>
    <file path="/home/user/raw_logs/active_connections.tsv"><![CDATA[
HOSTNAME\tIP\tPORT\tSTATE\tPID
web01\t10.0.0.1\t80\tESTABLISHED\t2345
db01\t10.0.0.2\t5432\tESTABLISHED\t5432
cache01\t10.0.0.3\t6379\tLISTEN\t3333
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/raw_logs/cpu_load.tsv"><![CDATA[
HOSTNAME\t1min\t5min\t15min
web01\t0.10\t0.12\t0.15
db01\t0.55\t0.53\t0.50
cache01\t0.20\t0.25\t0.30
]]></file>
  </pre_existing_files>

  <!-- Files the agent must create -->
  <expected_output_files>
    <file path="/home/user/reports/connection_load_summary.tsv"><![CDATA[
HOSTNAME\tPORT\tSTATE\t1min_LOAD
web01\t80\tESTABLISHED\t0.10
db01\t5432\tESTABLISHED\t0.55
cache01\t6379\tLISTEN\t0.20
]]></file>

    <!-- summary_commands.log must include at least the three keywords as separate commands -->
    <file path="/home/user/reports/summary_commands.log">
      <!-- we cannot know the exact commands the agent writes, only patterns: -->
      • file must contain at least two different lines that include the substring “cut”
      • file must contain at least one line that includes the substring “paste”
    </file>
  </expected_output_files>
name: task_000000_bd616d83
